question.en: Differentiating Rational and Irrational Numbers Determine whether each of the following numbers is rational or irrational. If it is rational, determine whether it is a terminating or repeating decimal. ŌōÉ 25 25 Ōōæ 33 9 33 9 ŌōÆ 11 11 Ōōō 17 34 17 34 Ōōö 0.3033033303333ŌĆ” 0.3033033303333ŌĆ” Solution ŌōÉ 25 : 25 : This can be simplified as 25 =5. 25 =5. Therefore, 25 25 is rational. Ōōæ 33 9 : 33 9 : Because it is a fraction of integers, 33 9 33 9 is a rational number. Next, simplify and divide. 33 9 = 33 11 9 3 = 11 3 =3. 6 ┬» 33 9 = 33 11 9 3 = 11 3 =3. 6 ┬» So, 33 9 33 9 is rational and a repeating decimal. ŌōÆ 11 : 11 11 : 11 is irrational because 11 is not a perfect square and 11 11 cannot be expressed as a fraction. Ōōō 17 34 : 17 34 : Because it is a fraction of integers, 17 34 17 34 is a rational number. Simplify and divide. 17 34 = 17 1 34 2 = 1 2 =0.5 17 34 = 17 1 34 2 = 1 2 =0.5 So, 17 34 17 34 is rational and a terminating decimal. Ōōö 0.3033033303333ŌĆ” 0.3033033303333ŌĆ” is not a terminating decimal. Also note that there is no repeating pattern because the group of 3s increases each time. Therefore it is neither a terminating nor a repeating decimal and, hence, not a rational number. It is an irrational number.
